body_txt: As is true for most stories , there are Characters within them, of varying flavors and types that populate their settings and drive or are driven by their plots. They can range from being the Protagonist of the story, to their Villain , and anything in between. However, one thing remains true for most stories: This Is Reality . And naturally, this extends to the nature of the characters themselves, who are treated as real entities existing within the world created by the story. Well, This Is Not That Trope . This type of reveal comes when a character in a story is revealed to not actually exist within the setting itself. As to the true nature of the character themselves, they might have been a Hologram , an an elaborate illusion (magical or otherwise) or an Eldritch Abomination implanting Fake Memories onto the settings populace. It may be used to explore psychological themes relating to memory or the illusory nature of reality, or it can be used to surprise the audience by twisting with how they engaged in the fictional work. Compare Noticing the Fourth Wall , where characters realize they aren't real in a different sense. Also "Scooby-Doo" Hoax , Imaginary Real Person and Scary Shadow Fakeout . Contrast Real After All . Related to Dead All Along (which is because the not-real character in question used to be real and is dead at the time of the reveal). Not-So-Imaginary Friend is a character you thought wasn't real but was real all along. If a character finds this out about themselves, it may very well be a Tomato in the Mirror . May be part of a larger reveal that All Just a Dream .
